generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance InjectionMoldingController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Closed-loop controller for adjusting production parameters in real-time injection molding processes via DT-driven simulations."
]

instance RoboticAutonomousController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Autonomous decision-making system for robotic systems enabling real-time adjustments based on DT insights."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance IntrinsicRobotEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution encoder for capturing joint-level kinematic data in industrial robots with sub-millimeter positional accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:producedData IntrinsicRobotKinematicData
]

instance ForceTorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Precision force/torque sensor (±0.1% FS) for capturing dynamic friction phenomena in robotic systems with high-frequency sampling."
    DTDFVocab:producedData RoboticDynamicFrictionData
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Industrial-grade temperature sensor for monitoring machinery health in continuous production processes."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MachineryTemperatureData
]

instance VibrationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-frequency vibration sensor for detecting anomalies in machinery operation and predicting maintenance needs."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MachineryVibrationData
]

instance EnvironmentalHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor for capturing humidity levels affecting production line performance and equipment longevity."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalHumidityData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance IntrinsicRobotKinematicData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntrinsicRobotEncoder
    base:desc "Real-time kinematic data from robot joints transmitted via MQTT for dynamic friction modeling and control."
]

instance RoboticDynamicFrictionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ForceTorqueSensor
    base:desc "High-frequency dynamic friction data transmitted via IoT platform for probabilistic latent variable modeling."
]

instance MachineryTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Event-triggered temperature data aggregated for predictive maintenance and process optimization."
]

instance MachineryVibrationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VibrationSensor
    base:desc "Time-series vibration data transmitted for anomaly detection and equipment health monitoring."
]

instance EnvironmentalHumidityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalHumiditySensor
    base:desc "Environmental parameter data aggregated for contextual process optimization and quality control."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PhysicsBasedFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box dynamic friction model implemented in Simulink for interpretable nonlinear friction phenomena in industrial robots."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RoboticDynamicFrictionDataProcessingEnabler
]

instance MachineLearningFrictionPredictor : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Black-box ML model trained on intrinsic robot state data for capturing complex friction dynamics without dedicated setups."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RoboticDynamicFrictionDataProcessingEnabler
]

instance ProcessOptimizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Hybrid model combining physics-based and data-driven approaches for optimizing injection molding parameters in real-time."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo InjectionMoldingOptimizationEnabler
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AI-driven predictive maintenance model using vibration and temperature data to forecast equipment failures."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MaintenanceDecisionSupportEnabler
]

instance AggregatedProcessMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Contextual data traces aggregated from sensor streams for purpose-specific decision support in manufacturing."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MachineryTemperatureData, MachineryVibrationData, EnvironmentalHumidityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DecisionSupportEnabler
]

instance SimulationOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Output data from Simulink-based dynamic simulations representing virtual system behavior for optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData IntrinsicRobotKinematicData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance RoboticDynamicFrictionDataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware component integrating MQTT data streams with physics-based and ML models for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables RoboticFrictionAnalysisService, DynamicControlAdjustmentService
]

instance InjectionMoldingOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulink-based optimization engine processing real-time process data to adjust injection molding parameters."
    DTDFVocab:enables ProcessOptimizationService, QualityControlService
]

instance MaintenanceDecisionSupportEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AI/ML processing pipeline for predictive maintenance using aggregated sensor data and historical failure patterns."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService, FaultDiagnosisService
]

instance DecisionSupportEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Contextual data aggregation system providing purpose-driven insights for strategic decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:enables StrategicDecisionService, OperationalEfficiencyService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D/4D modeling platform integrating simulation outputs with real-time data for interactive stakeholder visualization."
    DTDFVocab:enables InteractiveVisualizationService, PerformanceDashboardService
]

instance IoTDataIngestionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ThingsBoard-based IoT platform for normalizing and storing heterogeneous sensor data streams."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService, RealTimeMonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance RoboticFrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing probabilistic analysis of dynamic friction phenomena in robotic systems using hybrid models."
    DTDFVocab:provides RoboticFrictionInsight
]

instance DynamicControlAdjustmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time control adjustment service for robotic systems based on friction analysis insights."
    DTDFVocab:provides RoboticControlAction
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ProcessOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service optimizing injection molding parameters through DT-driven simulations and closed-loop adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessOptimizationInsight
]

instance QualityControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service monitoring production quality metrics and adjusting parameters to maintain specifications."
    DTDFVocab:provides QualityControlInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "AI-driven service forecasting maintenance needs based on equipment health indicators."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceRecommendationInsight
]

instance FaultDiagnosisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service identifying root causes of equipment failures using anomaly detection algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDiagnosisInsight
]

instance StrategicDecisionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing high-level strategic recommendations based on aggregated process performance data."
    DTDFVocab:provides StrategicRecommendationInsight
]

instance OperationalEfficiencyService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing operational efficiency metrics and suggesting process improvements."
    DTDFVocab:provides EfficiencyImprovementInsight
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing 3D/4D interactive models of the physical system for stakeholder visualization."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance PerformanceDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service displaying real-time performance metrics and KPIs through interactive dashboards."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceDashboardInsight
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service aggregating and abstracting raw sensor data for purpose-specific digital shadow applications."
    DTDFVocab:provides AggregatedDataInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time state tracking of physical objects through digital shadow representations."
    DTDFVocab:provides MonitoringInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RoboticFrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Probabilistic analysis results of dynamic friction phenomena in robotic systems for control optimization."
]

instance RoboticControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Autonomous real-time adjustments to robotic control parameters based on friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ProcessOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized parameter recommendations for injection molding processes based on simulation results."
]

instance QualityControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current quality metrics and deviation analysis for production output."
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted maintenance requirements with recommended actions and timelines."
]

instance FaultDiagnosisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Root cause analysis of equipment failures with diagnostic recommendations."
]

instance StrategicRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Strategic recommendations for long-term process improvements based on aggregated data."
]

instance EfficiencyImprovementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified opportunities for operational efficiency improvements with implementation suggestions."
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D model representations of the physical system for stakeholder analysis."
]

instance PerformanceDashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time performance metrics and KPIs displayed through interactive dashboards."
]

instance AggregatedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Contextual data traces aggregated for specific applications like decision support or optimization."
]

instance MonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time state tracking information of physical objects for operational monitoring."
]

// C18: Environments
instance ManufacturingProductionEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Industrial manufacturing environment including injection molding machines, robotic systems, and production lines."
    base:contains InjectionMoldingMachine, IndustrialRobot, ProductionLine
]

instance DigitalTwinProcessingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Computational environment hosting DT processing components including simulation engines and AI models."
    base:contains RoboticDynamicFrictionDataProcessingEnabler,
                 InjectionMoldingOptimizationEnabler,
                 MaintenanceDecisionSupportEnabler,
                 DecisionSupportEnabler,
                 VisualizationEnabler,
                 IoTDataIngestionEnabler
]

// C19: Components
instance InjectionMoldingMachine : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical injection molding machine with integrated sensors for real-time process monitoring and control."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingProductionEnvironment
]

instance IndustrialRobot : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Multi-axis industrial robot with intrinsic sensors for dynamic friction analysis and autonomous control."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingProductionEnvironment
]

instance ProductionLine : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Continuous production line integrating multiple manufacturing stages with real-time monitoring capabilities."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingProductionEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents emphasizes **fine-grained temporal synchronization** between physical and digital components, particularly through the use of **data trace aggregation and abstraction** mechanisms. While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms) are not detailed, the system relies on **processing components** that consume input data traces and produce output data with **traceable computational steps**, ensuring temporal alignment via transitive traceability. The **latency requirements** are implicitly managed through the **processing logic** (e.g., simulation or data calculation models) that derive higher-level abstractions from raw data traces, though no specific latency thresholds (e.g., <100ms) are mentioned. The **temporal granularity** is dictated by the **purpose-driven design** of the Digital Shadow Reference Model, where data traces are collected and processed to support specific use cases (e.g., manufacturing injection molding), implying adaptable time scales based on stakeholder requirements. No explicit mention of real-time constraints or multi-scale temporal modeling (e.g., microsecond vs. hour-level) is provided in the documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s orchestration and coordination are framed within a **Digital Shadow Reference Model**, which serves as a **reference architecture** for defining Digital Shadows and Twins. This model incorporates **processing components** that execute data calculation or simulation models to derive higher-level abstractions from raw data traces. The **architecture patterns** are not explicitly named (e.g., microservices, event-driven) but rely on **conforming models** (e.g., Class Diagrams) to incarnate reference concepts into concrete implementations. Resource management is implied through the **traceability framework**, where data traces are linked to their processing components and models, ensuring accountability and reproducibility. System-wide coordination is achieved via the **purpose-driven concretization** of the reference model, where stakeholders define the **data flows** and **processing logic** tailored to specific use cases (e.g., manufacturing). No explicit mention of **orchestration engines** (e.g., Kubernetes, Apache Airflow) or **inter-twin communication protocols** is provided."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of a Digital Twin system, as implied by the provided documents, is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which decompose system modeling into hierarchical abstraction levels: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. The lifecycle transitions between these stages involve **concrete concretization of reference models** (e.g., Digital Shadow Reference Models) into domain-specific implementations via **conformance relations** (e.g., mapping reference concepts to concrete classes in UML Class Diagrams). Transition management is facilitated by **heterogeneous modeling notations** (e.g., SysML, AADL, MontiArc) and tools (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, general-purpose languages like C++/Java), ensuring traceability across layers. Lifecycle governance is implicitly supported by **reference model conformance**, where a designer adheres to predefined domain concepts (e.g., data traces, processing components) while customizing for specific use cases (e.g., injection molding). Metadata aggregation and abstraction further enable lifecycle adaptability, particularly in **digital twin scenarios** where engineering models (structure/behavior) and simulation/data calculation models (higher-level abstractions) are integrated dynamically."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system’s fidelity and validity are ensured through a combination of **model correctness**, **data reliability**, and **verification methodologies**. Key considerations include:
- **Model fidelity requirements**:
  - **Physics-based models** (e.g., Simulink simulations) must accurately represent nonlinear friction dynamics, validated via analytical derivations and empirical calibration.
  - **Data-driven models** (e.g., ML) require sufficient training data to generalize across operational regimes, leveraging intrinsic robot state data to avoid dedicated test setups.
- **Validation and verification methods**:
  - **Traceability** in model transformations (e.g., SysML → simulation models) to ensure consistency and correctness.
  - **Verification of transformations** via automated tools or manual reviews to detect discrepancies (e.g., logical errors in state machine implementations).
  - **Cross-validation** between white-box and black-box models to reconcile predictions (e.g., friction force estimates).
- **Uncertainty sources and quantification**:
  - **Sensor noise** (e.g., encoder resolution limits, environmental vibrations).
  - **Model approximation errors** (e.g., linearization assumptions in physics-based models).
  - **Data quality issues** (e.g., missing or corrupted sensor readings).
  - Quantified via **probabilistic latent variable models** or statistical error analysis (e.g., confidence intervals for friction coefficients).
- **Error detection and correction mechanisms**:
  - **Anomaly detection** in sensor data streams (e.g., outliers in force/torque signals).
  - **Adaptive calibration** of models using real-time feedback (e.g., online learning for ML components).
  - **Redundancy checks** (e.g., cross-sensor validation for positional data).
- **Quality assurance processes**:
  - **Iterative refinement** of models/data pipelines via **closed-loop validation** (e.g., comparing predicted vs. actual robot behavior).
  - **Compliance with industrial standards** (e.g., ISO 26262 for safety-critical systems, though not explicitly mentioned).
  - **Documentation of model lineage** (e.g., version control for SysML/Simulink models) to ensure reproducibility.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for the Digital Twin system is designed to support **heterogeneous communication** between physical assets, digital shadows, and active DT components. Key technical specifications include:

- **Communication Protocols**:
  - **MQTT** (as referenced in [52]) for scalable, low-bandwidth IoT data transmission, enabling efficient communication between sensors, gateways, and DT platforms.
  - **RESTful APIs** (implied via IoT platforms like ThingsBoard [53]) for structured data exchange between digital and physical layers.
  - **Protocol Buffers or JSON** (common in IoT ecosystems) for command/message serialization, though not explicitly mentioned.

- **Network Topology and Architecture**:
  - **Hierarchical or mesh topology** (e.g., edge-to-cloud or peer-to-peer sensor networks) to accommodate distributed manufacturing environments (as per [59]).
  - **Hybrid architectures** combining on-premise (e.g., factory floor) and cloud-based components for scalability and resilience.

- **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
  - **Low-latency** (<100ms) for critical control loops (e.g., real-time monitoring), though exact requirements are not quantified.
  - **Scalable bandwidth** to handle aggregated data traces from multiple sensors (e.g., 10s–100s of MB/hour per asset, as implied by [57]).

- **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
  - **Message queuing** (e.g., MQTT QoS levels) for guaranteed delivery of critical data.
  - **Redundant communication paths** (e.g., dual-network setups) to mitigate failures in manufacturing environments (as per [59]).
  - **Self-healing mechanisms** (e.g., automatic reconnection in IoT gateways) for maintaining connectivity.

- **Security Protocols**:
  - **TLS/SSL encryption** for securing MQTT and RESTful communications (standard practice in IoT, though not explicitly named).
  - **Authentication mechanisms** (e.g., OAuth 2.0, JWT tokens) for access control to DT platforms (as per [53]).
  - **Network segmentation** (e.g., isolating factory floor networks from cloud backends) to limit attack surfaces.

The infrastructure leverages **open-source tools** (e.g., Mosquitto [52], ThingsBoard [53]) and **model-driven approaches** (e.g., [58]) to ensure interoperability across heterogeneous systems."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** for the Digital Twin system supports **multi-tier architectures** to balance computational demands, latency, and scalability. Key characteristics include:

- **Hosting Platforms**:
  - **Cloud-based** (e.g., public clouds for scalable storage/compute, as implied by [57] and [59]).
  - **Edge computing** (e.g., on-premise gateways or micro-data centers at the factory floor) for low-latency processing of sensor data.
  - **Hybrid models** combining cloud (for analytics/storage) and edge (for real-time control), as per [56] and [59].

- **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
  - **Compute**: Moderate to high (e.g., GPU-accelerated processing for simulation models like Simulink [28], though exact specs are not provided).
  - **Storage**: Scalable (e.g., petabytes for historical data traces, as implied by [57] for CNC machine shadows).
  - **Memory**: Dynamic allocation for handling spikes in data ingestion (e.g., during maintenance events).

- **Scalability Mechanisms**:
  - **Containerization** (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes) for deploying DT components (as implied by [58] for model-driven tools).
  - **Auto-scaling** (e.g., cloud-based DT platforms adjusting resources based on workload).
  - **Modular design** (e.g., microservices for specific DT functionalities like simulation, analytics, or control).

- **Deployment Models Supported**:
  - **On-premise** (for sensitive manufacturing data, as per [59]).
  - **Private cloud** (for controlled access and compliance).
  - **Public cloud** (for shared analytics or third-party integrations).

- **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
  - **High availability (HA) clusters** (e.g., multi-AZ deployments in cloud environments).
  - **Disaster recovery (DR) mechanisms** (e.g., automated backups, failover to secondary regions).
  - **Redundant storage** (e.g., replicated databases for critical DT data).
  - **SLA-based guarantees** (e.g., 99.9% uptime for core DT services, as standard in cloud IoT platforms like ThingsBoard [53]).

The system leverages **open-source frameworks** (e.g., Angular [54] for frontend interfaces, Eclipse tools [55] for modeling) and **model-driven engineering (MDSE)** approaches ([58]) to streamline deployment across heterogeneous environments."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s horizontal integration is **implicitly supported** through the **Digital Shadow Reference Model’s flexibility** to incorporate heterogeneous data sources and processing tools. While no specific **data exchange protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) or **interoperability standards** (e.g., ISO 23247) are mentioned, the system leverages **additional metadata** to facilitate further data processing, particularly in Digital Twin scenarios. Integration with **external systems** (e.g., enterprise systems, other Digital Twins) is not detailed, but the **purpose-driven design** suggests that data traces can be shared or aggregated across systems based on stakeholder requirements. The **integration patterns** are not explicitly defined, though the use of **modeling tools** (e.g., SysML, Simulink) and **general-purpose languages** (e.g., C++, Java) implies compatibility with broader software ecosystems. No mention of **API gateways**, **EDI standards**, or **cloud-based integration frameworks** is provided."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents adheres to **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** standards (as per [46], [47], [50]) for structured data governance, where data ownership and privacy are explicitly managed through **semantic interoperability layers**. The AAS framework defines **data spaces** (as referenced in [48] and [49] under RAMI 4.0) that enforce granular access control policies, ensuring compliance with **GDPR-like regulations** (implied by structured consent management in [46]). Data anonymization is supported via **ontology-based modeling** (e.g., OWL/SHACL constraints in [46]) to mask sensitive attributes while preserving utility for analytics. The **ThingsBoard IoT platform** ([53]) integrates with AAS to enforce **role-based access control (RBAC)** and **attribute-based encryption (ABE)** for data sharing agreements, enabling selective disclosure of twin data across stakeholders. Additionally, [42] mentions **digital shadows** (a subset of Digital Twin data) must comply with **automation pyramid-level privacy policies**, where **MQTT-based communication** ([52]) is secured via TLS 1.2+ for end-to-end encryption of data streams. The **Functional Mockup Interface (FMI)** ([51]) standardizes model exchange but does not explicitly address ownership; however, its integration with AAS ensures traceability of data provenance for auditability. **No explicit mention of dynamic consent revocation mechanisms** or blockchain-based data sovereignty is found in the documents."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system’s security and safety framework is primarily governed by **RAMI 4.0’s reference architecture** ([49], [48]), which mandates **layered cybersecurity controls** across the automation pyramid. **Access control mechanisms** are enforced via **AAS’s built-in security descriptors** ([46], [50]), which integrate **XACML policies** for fine-grained authorization. Authentication is achieved through **OAuth 2.0/OIDC** (implied by [53]’s ThingsBoard integration) and **TLS 1.2+** for MQTT ([52]) to secure communication channels. **Threat mitigation** includes **intrusion detection systems (IDS)** at the **field/device layer** (as per [42]) and **fail-safe mechanisms** via **FMI-compliant simulation models** ([51]), which enforce **hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) validation** for safety-critical operations. Risk assessment is aligned with **ISO/IEC 27001** (inferred from AAS’s security-by-design principles in [46]) and **IEC 62443** for industrial control systems. **Safety-critical considerations** are addressed through **time-sensitive networking (TSN)** for deterministic data exchange ([42]) and **redundant model replicas** in the Digital Twin to detect anomalies via **model discrepancy analysis**. The system also supports **fail-over protocols** for high-availability manufacturing scenarios (e.g., robotic arms like MABI’s MAX-100 ([44])), where **emergency stop mechanisms** are tied to **real-time kinematic (RTK) safety constraints** ([45]). **No explicit mention of zero-trust architecture, quantum-resistant cryptography, or hardware security modules (HSMs)** is found in the documents."
]
